name: PostgreSQL Guard
description: Deep PostgreSQL protection: DROP DATABASE/TABLE/SCHEMA, TRUNCATE, unqualified DELETE, dropdb, and destructive pg_dump --clean restores. Complements Database Baseline with engine-specific coverage. Derived from the destructive_command_guard pattern library.

rules.name: Require access request for drop database
rules.description: DROP DATABASE permanently deletes the entire database (even with IF EXISTS). Verify and back up first. Safer alternative: `pg_dump -h {host} -U {user} {dbname} > backup.sql` — Create a full backup before dropping.

rules.name: Require access request for drop schema
rules.description: DROP SCHEMA permanently deletes the schema and all its objects (even with IF EXISTS). Safer alternative: `pg_dump -n {schema_name} {dbname} > schema_backup.sql` — Backup schema before dropping.

rules.name: Require access request for dropdb cli
rules.description: dropdb permanently deletes the entire database. Verify the database name carefully. Safer alternative: `pg_dump -h {host} -U {user} {dbname} > backup.sql` — Create a full backup before dropping.

rules.name: Require justification for drop table
rules.description: DROP TABLE permanently deletes the table (even with IF EXISTS). Verify and back up first. Safer alternative: `pg_dump -t {tablename} {dbname} > table_backup.sql` — Backup the table before dropping.

rules.name: Require justification for truncate table
rules.description: TRUNCATE permanently deletes all rows without logging individual deletions. Safer alternative: `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {tablename}` — Check how many rows would be deleted.

rules.name: Require justification for delete without where
rules.description: DELETE without WHERE clause deletes ALL rows. Add a WHERE clause or use TRUNCATE intentionally. Safer alternative: `DELETE FROM {tablename} WHERE {condition}` — Add a WHERE clause to limit deletion.

rules.name: Require justification for pg dump clean
rules.description: pg_dump --clean drops objects before creating them. This can be destructive on restore. Safer alternative: `pg_dump {dbname} > backup.sql` — Create backup without DROP statements.
